segment-text: (PROVISIONAL TITLE) Written by
Based on: "One of Us" by Asne Seierstad
Shooting Script 08-03-2018 EXT. RURAL NORWAY - WHITE FARMHOUSE - DAY
An isolated white farmhouse set within dense pine forest. CAPTION: 21st July 2011. 90 miles east of Oslo.
INT. WHITE FARMHOUSE - GARAGE - CONTINUOUS
A man (early 30s) fills industrial sacks with thick black powder. Diesel soaked fertiliser mixed with aluminium. Messy work. This is ANDERS BREIVIK and he is preparing for war.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - DUSK A small island on the eastern flank of LAKE TYRIFJORDEN, 38
miles from NORWAY'S capital city, OSLO.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FERRY DOCK - DUSK The early evening ferry from the mainland.

Crowds of excited teenagers arrive for the annual summer camp run by Norway's ruling Labour Party. Among them: VILJAR HANSSEN (17), a striking young man and to all his friends a natural leader. With him, his younger brother TORJE (14). They are met off the boat by MOTHER UTOYA (45). She has run the camp for 20 years.
Welcome back! Hey!
The boys head on up to the centre of the island. Stopping at a check-in desk.
INT/EXT. WHITE FARMHOUSE - VAN - EVENING

BREIVIK loads the sacks into the white van. Next the detonator and charges - which he packs in with pieces of old mattress. Then he threads a long black wire from the sacks into the driver's compartment.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - DUSK
Make-shift signs: Oslo; Finnmark; Nordland... They come from Northern Norway, so they make for the Troms & Svalbard camp. VILJAR and TORJE arrive at the campsite.
Viljar sees his best friends - Simon and Anders (both 17). They hug and begin pitching their tents.
So you brought a sleeping bag with you this time?
Yeah... my parents packed it.
All around: excitement at the weekend ahead.
EXT/INT. WHITE FARMHOUSE - EVENING
BREIVIK loading a black peli-case: A Glock. A rifle. A shotgun. Thousands of rounds of ammunition. Grenades. He exits the farmhouse with the peli-case and drives away.
EXT. CENTRAL OSLO - GOVERNMENT DISTRICT - EVENING
Angle on Norway's government buildings clustered together in the middle of the city, with a view of the harbour.
visit the AUF summer camp on Utoya. For your speech...
INT. GOVERNEMNT PM OFFICE - TOP FLOOR - EVENING
Angle through the window: Oslo skyline with PM STOLTENBERG soft left of frame. Prime Minister, here is your agenda
visiting the AUF summer camp on Utoya. Attached is a draft of your speech...
PM walks away towards the door with AIDE 3.
They move out into a corridor.
I'll do it in the Residency. Bring the Justice Minister earlier. (to office off screen) Have a good night. Goodnight Prime Minister.
(into radio) Coming down now. See you back here after lunch. Good night, Sir.
The lift doors closes.
INT/EXT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE LOBBY - EVENING
PM crosses the lobby. Past a SECURITY GUARD.
The GUARD watches him walk out to his waiting car. Weighed down by the burdens of power.
INT. PRIME MINISTER'S CAR - EVENING
See PM alone on the backseat.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CAMPSITE - EVENING A campfire. Music, socialising.
VILJAR, TORJE, SIMON and ANDERS around a fire pit. SOMEONE strumming a guitar. We see LARA RASHID (16) and her sister BANO (18) too.
EXT. RURAL NORWAY - MAIN ROAD - NIGHT
BREIVIK on the road towards OSLO.
EXT. WEST OSLO - BREIVIK'S MOTHER'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
He pulls up outside an apartment complex, in an up-market part of Oslo.
INT. MOTHER'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

BREIVIK comes out of the bathroom, freshly showered. LATER: BREIVIK and his mother eating dinner in front of the TV: economic stagnation and the fall-out from the 2008 banking crisis. Europe's growing migrant crisis.
It's good. A lot of work.
I've been thinking, it must be lonely up there...
I see people every day.
(gets up) I have an errand.
INT/EXT. MOTHER'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
Angle from a window: Breivik into a small Fiat Diablo. His mother watching him from behind the curtain, as he draws away in the Fiat.
EXT. FJORDS OUTSIDE OSLO - THE NEXT MORNING
A sailing boat cuts through the water.
INT. SAILING BOAT. BELOW DECK - EARLY MORNING
GEIR LIPPESTAD (40s) a criminal lawyer, making coffee on a stove.
Ready to get home?
REBEKAH's eyes light up.
(smiling) Me too.
EXT. SAILING BOAT - DECK - EARLY MORNING
LIPPESTAD comes up onto deck. Sees SIGNE, his wife. Hands her the coffee.
Better not... No?
Looks like I can't drink coffee for a while...
(finally gets it) Really? Really.
He starts laughing. They hug.
Oh my God. How did that happen?
(laughing) You've forgotten?
I need to get cases reassigned. I really want to take time off this time.
They round the headland towards Oslo.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CAMPSITE - MORNING
VILJAR wakes. Sees the rain. Wakes Torje next to him. They walk over to Simon and Anders' tent. Where did you get to last night?
(laughs) Swimming? With the enemy? They walk across the campsite to the canteen. We catch a
glimpse of PM Stoltenberg's photo, advertising his speech on
Viljar checks the seminar noticeboard. See: 'If I was PM...'
(reading) First seminar at 10. Football at 3.
Finnmark's big guy at the back is out.
How do you know that?
Skinny-dipping. With the enemy.
Laughter as they head into the canteen.
INT. BREIVIK'S BEDROOM - MOTHER'S APARTMENT - MORNING
BREIVIK at his computer. Writing an email: Consider this my personal gift to all Europeans. Anders Behring Breivik.
Leave it outside the door.
BREIVIK swallows a couple of pills. Highlights a file: "2083: A European Declaration of Independence." Presses "Send all"
INT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN/SEMINAR ROOM - DAY
OK, good morning everyone... Today is the first seminar. We'll start
with a little exercise, that I call
Viljar you can start. VILJAR laughs and then goes up to the stage.
OK, so in Svalbard everyone is welcome, regardless of nationality. We've got Norwegians, Russians, Chinese, Croatians. All living and working together.
Yes, but you only have a couple thousand people, how does that
translate across Europe? LARA and BANO enter. VILJAR sees.
It's the principle. Thats what's important and if I was Prime Minister, that's what I'd stand for...
INT. PM'S RESIDENCY - STUDY - DAY
PM STOLTENBERG is mid-way through a security briefing with: THE PST DIRECTOR, SECURITY OFFICIAL LARS, PM AIDE 2 OYSTEIN and SECURITY OFFICIAL 2 ENDRE.
massive surge in migrant numbers
Angle on the document: huge migrant figures from the Middle East and North Africa.
INT. BREIVIK'S BEDROOM - MOTHERS APARTMENT - DAY

BREIVIK naked. A police uniform laid out neatly before him, on his bed. He carefully pulls on each item of clothing. Attaches fake police insignia badges. Picks up a Glock pistol and straps it to his thigh. Stands back to admire himself in the mirror. He's ready.
INT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN/SEMINAR ROOM - DAY
VILJAR still in the seminar.
Prime Minister?
No. I'm just saying whether we like it or not, the world is on the move. We're part of a global system. Better to shape it, than pretend to pull up drawbridges that we can't sustain.
EXT. CENTRAL OSLO - DAY
Breivik's van drives towards the centre of Oslo.
INT. BREIVIK'S VAN - CONTINUOUS
Breivik driving. He stops at a red traffic light. His POV: Migrants crowded under blankets and tents.
EXT. GOVERNMENT BUILDING - OFFICE OF PM - DAY Breivik's van enters the Government district. Parks behind

the Office of the Prime Minister. Checks the time. Pulls out a lighter. Lights the long wire protruding from the back of the van. Puts on his black police helmet. As the fuse burns, he exits the van, locking the door gently. He calmly walks away.
INT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE LOBBY - CONTINUOUS
THE SECURITY GUARD sees the van on his CCTV screen. Watches the uniformed figure walk away.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FOOTBALL PITCH - DAY

VILJAR, TORJE, SIMON and ANDERS - dressed in football kit walk down to the field below the campsite. It's a mixed game. MOTHER UTOYA marshals crowds of TEENAGERS. Laughter and excitement, despite the rain. As VILJAR walks out onto the pitch, sees LARA.
EXT. GOVERNMENT DISTRICT STREET - DAY
BREIVIK walks around a corner, and gets into the Fiat Diablo.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FOOTBALL PITCH - DAY The football game in full-swing in the rain. MOTHER UTOYA
referees. TORJE watching from the sideline. VILJAR at the heart of the action. Scoring.
EXT. OSLO - MAIN ROAD NORTH - DAY
BREIVIK drives out of Oslo. Turns on the radio.
EXT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE REAR - SAME TIME
The van parked. Unsuspecting pedestrians. Norway oblivious.
INT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE LOBBY - SAME TIME
SECURITY GUARD on the phone to the DVLA. I'll check the number plate and I'll try to find the driver. He glances over to CCTV screen, where...
EXT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE REAR - SAME TIME
A second SECURITY GUARD is approaching the van. Suddenly: BOOM. The van explodes in a massive fireball.
INT. PM'S RESIDENCY - STUDY TO SECURE ROOM - SECONDS LATER 31
PM goes to the window. Sees a huge tower of smoke rising in the distance. Suddenly:
Sir, we need you to leave. Now! His SECURITY DETAIL drag him out of the room. More guards responding.
Lock down all exits! The PM is propelled through the kitchen. Someone gives him a bullet-proof vest. Down a stairwell.
Get the doors open!
Through a set of doors and into a bare situation room.
The doors close. The PM protected, but powerless.
EXT. GOVERNMENT OFFICE REAR - SAME TIME Government quarter devastated. Smoke, debris, paper cascading
through the air. A gaping hole in the ground where Breivik's van was parked. And from every direction the sound of sirens and screaming.
EXT. OSLO HARBOUR - SAME TIME
LIPPESTAD and SIGNE docking in Oslo Harbour, looking at a huge column of smoke rising above the city.
What is that??
On LIPPESTAD. He looks down at REBEKAH.
Let's get in the car.
EXT/INT. FIAT DOBLO/TUNNEL - ROAD TO UTOYA - SAME TIME
BREIVIK approaches the tunnel heading west out of the city.
EXT. GOVERNMENT BUILDING - REAR & LOBBY - SAME TIME
Blood-stained victims emerge. People screaming. Bodies on the ground. Police start to arrive. Staggered by the scale of the destruction.
INT. PM'S RESIDENCY- SECURE ROOM - SAME TIME
Chaos as the SECURE ROOM begins to fill with AIDES. The PM watches news coverage.
Bring up news of the explosion breaks on TV. Is this gas? A gas explosion?
On PM as he surveys pictures of the damage. On air speculation about the status of the government.
EXT/INT. FIAT DOBLO/TUNNEL - ROAD TO UTOYA - SAME TIME
BREIVIK's van comes out the tunnel. We're getting reports of an explosion in central Oslo... See BREIVIK. He looks down at his GPS. His destination: UTOYA ISLAND.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CAMPSITE/CANTEEN - DAY
VILJAR with TORJE, returning from the football. They see TEENAGERS by their tents, on their phones. Concerned faces.
What's going on?
An explosion in Oslo. Everyone's been called to the canteen.
VILJAR checks his phone as they head towards the building.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN - CONTINUOUS
We follow VILJAR and TORJE into the crowded canteen. SIMON and ANDERS too. Across the room: LARA and her sister BANO.
Quiet please! I'm going to shout because the microphone is down... Room becomes still.
TORJE looks up at VILJAR.
In the meantime, we're suspending activities until we know what's
going on. VILJAR pulls out his phone. Starts to dial. As kids question MOTHER UTOYA.
We will tell you as soon as we know more. Now please don't worry. This is Utoya - you're safe here.
(into phone)
EXT STREET/BUILDING - CENTRAL OSLO - SAME TIME
VILJAR'S PARENTS, CHRISTIN and SVEINN (30) emerge from a hotel door. The sound of sirens in the distance. Ahead of them, PEOPLE hurrying towards government centre. POLICE CARS passing.
(on phone) Hi, I'm fine. Dad's with me. We're at the hotel. We're fine.
(looking at his phone) They're saying it's an explosion.
Dad says it's some kind of explosion.
Was it a bomb?
We don't know.
It looks like it's over by the government centre.
Are you both OK? We're fine. Torje's a bit scared but I'll look after him.
Ok listen, well look there's no need to worry. Whatever it is the police are dealing with it. We'll call you when we know what's going on. OK?
She hangs up, as they keep walking towards whatever has happened. More EMERGENCY VEHICLES pass by.
They're fine.
They walk on.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN - CONTINUOUS
Viljar hangs up.
He smiles, ruffling his little brother's hair.
EXT STREET/BUILDING - CENTRAL OSLO - SAME TIME
CHRISTIN and SVEINN keep walking towards whatever has happened. More EMERGENCY VEHICLES pass by.
(dialing again) Everyone's down at Labour HQ for the campaign meeting. Everyone.
You should call them.

(phone answers) Lena? Are you OK? What's happened? (listens, then to Sveinn) Sounds like the whole government centre's been hit. (into phone) Have you heard from Erik and the others? ...OK. I'm going there now. Call me if you hear anything. Yes. Cancel all of today's meetings.
They walk on.
INT. BREIVIK'S MOTHERS APARTMENT - SAME TIME
BREIVIK'S MOTHER watches rolling news coverage of the bomb. She goes into Breivik's bedroom. Looks around. Calls his mobile.
EXT. ROAD TO UTOYA & MAINLAND FERRY DOCK - CONTINUOUS

BREIVIK driving. His phone rings: "Mother". He doesn't answer. Up ahead, a sign to UTOYA ISLAND. He takes the exit. Rounds a bend to reveal the beauty of LAKE TYRIFJORDEN. UTOYA ISLAND in the middle. He parks. Takes a pill. Sees a COUPLE of TEENAGE VOLUNTEERS in a high-vis jackets, standing by the jetty.
(approaching) Hi, I'm Martin Nilsen from OSLO P.D. There's been a bomb in the city. I've been sent to secure the island. They cancelled the ferry.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CAMPSITE - SAME TIME
MOTHER UTOYA is speaking to a huddle of TEENAGERS, trying to lighten the mood. TROND next to her. A call to her radio:
(to the guard) Ok, I'm coming. (to TROND) You stay here, I'll go and get him.
EXT. MAINLAND FERRY DOCK - SAME TIME
BREIVIK sees the FERRY leaving the island.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FERRY TRAVELLING - SAME TIME
MOTHER UTOYA on the foredeck. Sees the mainland.
EXT. MAINLAND FERRY DOCK - SAME TIME
BREIVIK watching the boat get closer.
EXT. MAINLAND FERRY DOCK - MOMENTS LATER
As the ferry ramp lowers, MOTHER UTOYA sees BREIVIK clearly for the first time. He's heavily armed and wearing what looks like a black combat suit.
Hi, I'm Monica, I'm in charge. What's going on?
Martin Nilsen. Oslo Police Department. I need you to take me to the island. Get everyone together and I'll give a debrief about the bomb. Everyone?
Yes, everyone. It's important.
What are the cases for?
My weapons.
Well you need to keep them covered up. They'll scare the children. Of course.
They start back towards the island.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CAMPSITE - SAME TIME
TROND gets a call.
TROND, walks down to meet them.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FERRY DOCK - MOMENTS LATER
The ferry docks. MOTHER UTOYA and BREIVIK disembark.
(to Breivik) This is Trond Berntsen, our head of security here on the island.
BREIVIK shakes hands.
Martin Nilsen. Oslo PD. They sent me to secure the island.
Well no-one called us.
Everyone's needed in Oslo. It's chaos down there. You should see it.
They start to walk up the hill.
(something about this not right) So which precinct are you from?
So you must know Jorn then? Isn't he down at PST?
Jorn? Sure. He's one of my superiors.
Is everyone gathered up there for the debrief?
We're calling them in now.
Trond stops. A moment between them. Who's lying here?
Do you mind. Can I see your ID again? Sure.
Suddenly, BREIVIK pulls out the Glock.
BANG! BANG! TROND falls. Then he turns the gun on MOTHER BANG! BANG!
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CAMPSITE - SAME TIME
Tight on VILJAR, as the sound of gunfire reverberates across the island. Around him TEENAGERS emerge from their tents. Confused looks.
EXT. FERRY DOCK - SAME TIME
BREIVIK on the move. Up the slope. Towards the campsite.
EXT. CAMPSITE - SAME TIME
More cracks. Closer this time.
I'll go and see what's happening. BREIVIK still coming. Fast.
EXT. Campsite - SAME TIME
VILJAR watching ANDERS walk up towards the canteen. More loud bangs. The sound of screaming on the wind.
BREIVIK approaching the top of the rise.
EXT. CAMPSITE - SAME TIME
VILJAR sees in the distance screaming TEENAGERS running towards their car. And behind them, BREIVIK cresting the hill. A moment suspended in time... as he raises his gun. RUN!
(realising)
VILJAR grabs TORJE and runs, as BREIVIK opens fire.
EXT. GOVERNMENT DISTRICT STREET - SAME TIME
Viljar's PARENTS, CHRISTIN and SVEINN, arrive at a corner just down from government centre. POLICE CARS, AMBULANCES. CROWDS milling around. Chaos.
(approaches a police officer) Was it a bomb? Move back! You have to move back!

Excuse me. Excuse me, Sir. I need to see my colleagues. We're supposed to be having a campaign meeting. At Labour Party headquarter. Please move back! You can't come through. We're clearing this area.
Can you at least tell me which buildings have been hit? We don't know. You just need to move back. Please.
Suddenly her phone rings... The sound of screaming, running, and the phone being fumbled through hands.
Mum, they're shooting at us! Viljar?? What'sThe phone line goes dead. What is it?
(trying to call them back) It was Viljar. He said someone was shooting at them.
Shooting? What do you mean shooting?
He just said there was shooting and hung up.
Try him back.
(dialing) I'm calling him...
Shooting what? What did he say exactly?
It was really quick. He definitely said shooting. (listens) It's going to voicemail.
Just someone is shooting?
(trying phone again) At us. He said: Mum. Someone's shooting at us. Quietly. Just like that. That was it. (checking his phone too) Did he sound frightened?
Yes. I heard gunshots.
They look at the chaos in front of them.
Gunshots? We need to get down there.
They push their way through the chaos and start running to their car.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - LOVER'S PATH - SAME TIME
Back to VILJAR and TORJE, running into the woods. Panic. Hundreds of TEENAGERS. Gunfire, screaming behind them.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN - SAME TIME
BREIVIK approaches the main building.
(Sees faces at the window) It's okay. Police. I've got it under control. Stay there and I'll come and help you.
He goes into the building.
INT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN CORRIDOR - CONTINUOUS
BREIVIK down a corridor.
INT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN - SAME TIME
As dozens of TEENAGERS crouch in corners. Waiting to be BREIVIK enters. Sees them. Raises his gun.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN - SAME TIME
The sound of gunfire and screaming from inside.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - LOVER'S PATH - SAME TIME

VILJAR and TORJE running. Looking for somewhere - anywhere to hide. VILJAR sees a break in a fence. They tumble down a muddy bank. Scramble sideways onto a ledge. A dead end. Below them an 8ft drop to the lake. They're trapped. Other TEENAGERS crawl onto the ledge behind them.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CANTEEN - SAME TIME
BREIVIK leaves the canteen. Crosses the campsite.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - LEDGE - SAME TIME71
VILJAR and TORJE crammed together on the ledge. The shooting getting louder. He sees SIMON. But no ANDERS.
Torje, look at me. Look at me. TORJE tears streaming down face. It's going to be ok..
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - LOVERS PATH - SAME TIME

BREIVIK down the path. Sees the gap in the fence ahead. Climbs through it. Sees them all clustered on the ledge. Lifts his gun... He opens fire. PEOPLE falling. Panic. Screaming. VILJAR and TORJE jump. They land on rocks at the water's edge and scramble for cover. BREIVIK shoots again. Simon hit. Two others as well. And then VILJAR is hit. He struggles back up, holding his shoulder. Another shot, he falls again. Another, still he keeps going. Desperately struggling to escape. And then one last impact punctures his skull. TORJE turns back to see his brother fall. VILJAR sees TORJE coming back for him. Go Mustering all his remaining strength he splashes water at
GO!
VILJAR (CONT'D)
TORJE turns and throws himself forward into cover. Through a haze of blood, he sees the dark figure looming above him on the clifftop, before he turns away into the trees.
INT. HONEFOSS POLICE STATION - DAY
A lone SWITCHBOARD OPERATOR. A quiet room. A red light on her switchboard begins to flash.
What's your emergencyThe sound of screaming down the end of the phone. Then it cuts. The SWITCHBOARD OPERATOR puts down the phone. Almost immediately, another red light. Help us! Please
Suddenly: all the lights on the switchboard turn red.
EXT. HONEFOSS POLICE STATION - CONTINUOUS
HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 and his team scrambling to Utoya.
INT. PM RESIDENCY - SECURE ROOM - DAY
Follow behind SECURITY OFFICIAL LARS on the phone as he approaches the PM - who is standing, watching news coverage, surrounded by several AIDES.
End on shot of monitor.
EXT. ROAD TO UTOYA ISLAND - DAY
HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 in his car. Blue lights, sirens.
Assumed terror attack. Possible multiple shooters. DELTA are enroute and will assume site command. Utoya Island comes into sight.
EXT. OSLO STREETS - DAY CHRISTIN and SVEINN driving towards Utoya.
CHRISTIN keeps trying Viljar's phone, turns on the radio.
EXT. CENTRAL OSLO - DAY GEIR LIPPESTAD and family driving towards the centre of Oslo.
Smoke clearly visible. Ambulances screech past. Police cars. All away from the bomb site.
What the hell is going on?
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FOREST AREA 1 - DAY
BREIVIK in tight close-up moving across the island. Killing methodically. Relentlessly. He launches a smoke grenade. Acrid orange smoke billows.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - VILJAR SHORE - SAME TIME

VILJAR lying on the rocks. Screaming and gunfire in the distance. The sounds of sobbing and pain nearby. He uses his uninjured hand to touch his wounds. Too many to stem the loss of blood. He reaches his head. Feels the flesh of his exposed brain.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - CLIFFS - SAME TIME
TORJE reaches the shadow of the cliffs. KIDS hiding, including LARA. He looks to the mainland - it's too far. Sudden gunfire above him. He recoils into the rock.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - ROAD / MAINLAND FERRY DOCK - SAME TIME 70

HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 takes the turning for Utoya. Down the steep slope. Sees Utoya ahead in the distance. He pulls up at the jetty. He gets out and looks over to Utoya Island. In the distance: dirty orange smoke billowing through the pine trees. Volleys of gunfire echoing across the water. Figures in the lake desperately swimming for their lives. On HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 as he takes this in: My God... HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1
Storoya?? But that's a mile away!
HONEFOSS POLICEMAN#1 sprints back to his car. Knows time
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FOREST AREA 2 - SAME TIME
BREIVIK sees the POLICE on the mainland. Sees a discarded mobile phone. Dials... Police Emergency Line...
Hi. This is Anders Behring Breivik. I am a Commander in the Norwegian Anti-Communist Resistance Movement. Who?
Anders Behring Breivik. I'm on Utoya. I've just finished my operation and I would like to give myself up. Utoya?
The line cuts. BREIVIK drops the phone. Moves on towards more targets. We see that this is all part of his plan.
EXT. ROAD TO UTOYA (NEAR SOLLIHOGDA)- DAY CHRISTIN and SVEINN speeding to UTOYA.
The lake comes into view. And a police roadblock. SVEINN slams on the brakes. They get out of car. The sound of gunfire in the distance.
My children are on the island! You've got to let me through. You can't Ma'am. This is an active situation. It's not safe.
Suddenly, POLICE vehicles approach at high speed. Then ambulances. As they roar past, CHRISTIN makes a break for it on foot.
ROADBLOCK POLICEMAN (CONT'D)
The POLICEMAN follows. Catches her.
Christin. Christin! No..
He takes her back to the car.
We'll find another way through.
He pulls a hard U-turn and they speed back the way they came.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - VILJAR SHORE - SAME TIME
VILJAR lies helpless. Gunfire in the distance. He moves his head. Sees the outline of lifeless bodies.
EXT. MAINLAND - GOLF COURSE JETTY - DAY HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 arrives at RV point. DELTA UNITS

arriving. The sound of distant gunfire throughout. Unit A to the south end of the island, B to the north. (NB Correct terminology tbc) A police RHIB is frantically deployed. But it's overloaded. The engine cuts out.
HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1
He spots a civilian boat approaching. OFFICERS unload into it. More precious moments wasted.
EXT. MAINLAND - LAKE - SAME TIME
A handful of leisure boats speed away from the mainland towards the island.
EXT. UTOYA SLAND - FOREST AREA 3 - SAME TIME
BREVIK sees them coming.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - TORJE SHORE - SAME TIME
So does TORJE. As they approach, he throws himself into the water and swims out from the cover of the cliffs, towards them.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - FOREST AREA 3 - SAME TIME
BREIVIK sees TORJE. Opens fire. The tourist boat swerves. TORJE disappears under the surface. Swimming for his life.
EXT. LAKE - DAY
HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 behind the wheel of the second boat. Feels the engine straining. Willing it forward. The sound of gunfire ahead.
- FOREST AREA 3 - SAME TIME
BREIVIK ducks into cover, reloads, moves back across the island.
EXT. LAKE - SAME TIME
TORJE still swimming. The sound of an engine behind him. He turns to see a second boat, the DRIVER waving frantically. TORJE is pulled onboard.
(as the boat speeds away) No. Wait. We have to go back for my brother! More gunfire. (steering the boat away) We can't! We've got to go!
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - VILJAR SHORE - SAME TIME
VILJAR fighting to stay awake. He starts to sing...
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - DELTA BOAT - DAY
HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 speeds towards the island and catches a glimpse of BREIVIK in the trees. One shooter. Two o'clock.
EXT/INT. LIPPESTAD'S HOME. DAY

LIPPESTAD and FAMILY pull up outside their home. LIPPESTAD unloads REBEKAH from the car, as SIGNE ferries the other kids inside. We follow them in. Through into the living room where SIGNE turns on the TV: Aerial footage above Utoya Island. Breaking news of suspected shooting.
(pulling out phone) I'm calling the hospital...
On LIPPESTAD still watching the TV, as she dials.
(into the phone) Hi. This is Signe. I'm watching the news. Do you need me to come in? (listens) I'll standby.
She hangs up.
(to LIPPESTAD) They're calling everyone in. They'll let me know as soon as they've designated a hospital.
LIPPESTAD nods as she runs upstairs.
INT. PM RESIDENCY - SECURE ROOM - SAME TIME
The PM on a conference call. Grappling to make sense of the unfolding attack. Are DELTA on the island yet? Where the hell are they?
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - MAIN JETTY - DAY HONEFOSS POLICEMAN #1 lands at the ferry dock.

Do you know or do you think you know?
I know. And there's a difference.
Do you think people can read your mind?
Sometimes. I think they try.
Did you feel like that before the attacks, or is it a new feeling?
It's not new. But it got worse when I was planning the attacks.
Worse?
Much worse.
See LIPPESTAD watched BREIVIK. Wonders if this is all an act.
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - VILJAR'S NEW ROOM - DAY
VILJAR in his hospital bed. A knock at the door.
Hi. What are you doing around here?
I live around here. We're checking on survivors. How you feeling?
I'm OK. (recognises her) So you made it off the island OK?
Yeah. I was in the shower block when it started. I managed to run and hide. (beat) But I got separated from my sister Bano. She didn't make it.
I'm so sorry. Yeah... I'm sorry about Simon and Anders too. You were really close, right?
Yeah we were. It's shit.
It sits there. Survival is a strange land for both of them.
That's shit too... Actually it's really shit.
Want me to smuggle you something in?
VILJAR shakes his head.
Nice...Except I don't smoke.
He laughs. The first time since the attack. But then pain. He puts his right hand to his head.
No, it's just, I have to be careful. It's going to take time. Yeah, that's what everyone keeps saying.
Yeah, that's what they say to me too. (a moment between them) I'll leave you in peace. But I can come back if you want? Sure. That'd be nice. Great. See you. Take care.
And she's gone - leaving VILJAR alone.
INT. COURTHOUSE - JUDGES CHAMBER - DAY
We follow LIPPESTAD into the chambers. He looks across at INGA and SVEINN, as the JUDGE enters. The psychiatric report determines
Thank you, Your Honour.
See LIPPESTAD. A first victory.
INT. ILA PRISON - HOLDING ROOM - DAY
LIPPESTAD watching BREIVIK reading his psychiatric report.
This is only the diagnosis. It will be up to the court whether to accept it. (MORE)

(hands him a bundle of newspapers, letters) In the meantime, the judge has modified the terms of your detention. BREIVIK scans the headlines - furious denunciations of his insanity plea. Opens the letters. Death threats.
And I will be allowed to speak at the trial?
This hasn't been decided yet.
It's very important. (hands LIPPESTAD a list) There are witnesses I need to crossexamine. LIPPESTAD scans a who's-who of Norway, starting with the King. Then the Prime Minister.
Anders, you can't call the Prime Minister. Norway isn't on trial. You are.
Are you sure about that? See LIPPESTAD, as the sounds of a crowd builds.
INT. PM'S RESIDENCY - NIGHT
The PM and his AIDES enter the hallway. Through the window, the PM sees the waiting press outside.
See PM. He nods as he puts on his coat. Straightens his tie.
PM picks up his bag.
PM exits through the main door to...
EXT. RESIDENCY - CONTINUOUS
Flashlights. A waiting PRESS POOL, and a lobby of questions:
Prime Minister! Any comment on Mr Breivik's insanity plea?

Is Breivik making a mockery of justice, Prime Minister? How do you respond to ordinary Norwegians who think Mr. Breivik should be made accountable for his actions? Like all Norwegians, I'm following the legal proceedings closely, but it would be inappropriate for me, as Prime Minister, to comment on an on-going court case. What about the elections, Prime Minister? Are you going to postpone them? The elections will go ahead as scheduled. It is important that our democratic process continues as

Do you honestly feel we can have elections so soon after the attacks? When there is so much anger? We're being tested as never before. In our courts. In our hearts, and in our minds. We have to trust our system. Trust that it will find a way to bring us through safely. Together. And I believe it will.
The PM gets into his waiting car.
EXT. COURTHOUSE - DAY
A GROUP of LAWYERS, led by FAMILY LAWYER (female, 40s) METTE LARSEN, walk towards court through a crowd of JOURNALISTS.

I can say on behalf of my clients, that they strongly oppose Mr. Breivik's psychiatric diagnosis, and as the coordinating lawyer for the families and survivors of the 22nd July attacks, I will be petitioning the court today to order further assessments. We demand that Mr. Breivik face justice and be held accountable for his actions.
Flurry of flashlights.
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - VILJAR'S NEW ROOM - DAY
A NURSE carefully removes VILJAR'S bandages on his shoulder. Revealing angry red wounds. CHRISTIN watches. Sees VILJAR is in pain.
(to nurse) Can we get him something for the-
No. No more morphine.
LATER: CHRISTIN feeds VILJAR. Dabs his mouth with a cloth. VILJAR silent, submissive.
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR TO MEETING AREA - DAY
CHRISTIN pushes VILJAR in the wheelchair. Her phone rings. She turns it onto silent.
I'm not leaving you. So how does that work on election night?
I can Skype. Skype? Seriously?
Yeah, Skype. Maybe you could Skype with me? If you're up to it...
Only if you win...
(the first time she's seen him smile) I'll try.
Her phone goes again.
I'll call them later. It's a fucking election. Take it.

A moment between them. She takes the call, leaving VILJAR. He looks around, uncertain in the BUSY corridor. Then he hears the sound of a TV. Wheels towards it... ...Norwegians are convinced Breivik is trying to cheat justice, and have reacted with an outpouring of national anger. He kills 77 people and doesn't go
Norwegian. I feel so sorry for those parents. The thought of him walking the
VILJAR turns a corner. Comes face-to-face with BREIVIK on the TV.
EXT. UTOYA ISLAND - VILJAR SHORE - A FLASHBACK
Gunfire. Running. Screaming. And that DARK FIGURE looming over the clifftop. Viljar!
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - CORRIDOR TO MEETING AREA - SAME TIME
(bringing him back to reality) Viljar! See VILJAR. Battling fear and rage.
He can't get you. He's never going to get you.
INT. PM'S RESIDENCY - LIVING ROOM - NIGHT
The PM watching election results. Alone. The mood is sombre. The Labour Party retain power, but the Right have made striking gains across the country.
...there is no question Norway is following the European trend. Hardline right-wing parties are growing
in strength... and the question has
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - VILJAR'S NEW ROOM - NIGHT
CHRISTIN on her laptop. Her family around her.

(to Skype camera) I'm honoured to become Mayor of Svalbard, and I want to thank everyone for being so understanding. It's been a difficult time, but hopefully we will be home soon. (beat) Oh and Viljar has a message.
She adjusts the computer.
(steps in) So tonight we're going to have a little celebration here in the hospital. And in the morning I'll get to work, because we have so much to do to heal our country.
She ends the call. The room subdued, the whole family strangely empty.
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - PHYSIO ROOM - MORNING

VILJAR trying to stand. A PHYSIOTHERAPIST assisting. His good hand pushes down hard into the bench. His legs shake as he forces his body up. Steady... VILJAR takes a step. Then his legs buckle. He shouts out in pain. The PHYSIO catches him. Close on VILJAR, all energy spent, angry and afraid.
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL GARDENS - DAY
VILJAR walking slowly with a frame. Managing the pain as best he can. Lara next to him.
You want to stop? Go slowly...
And I get flashbacks.. Do you get the fucking flashbacks?

Yeah. I had one yesterday. Some people across the street started to run. For a bus or something completely ordinary. But I started to run too. I don't know why, but I felt like he was right behind me, over my shoulder, he was this big...
(interrupting) 20-foot high. With a huge gun. Just fucking huge. High above you, right?
Yeah. Exactly... (beat) I just kept running.
That's easy for you to say... Right! Sorry.
No, it's OK - you want to race?
They laugh. Later: they sit in the hospital cafe. VILJAR looks over to a newspaper picture of BREIVIK.
They're saying he's insane. Do you think he's insane? I don't know. Maybe.
I saw him like This close. (beat) He knew what he was doing. Definitely.
What are you going to do? When you get out?
I don't know... I can't see the future. It's like I'm still there on the rocks. Like I'm out there in no man's land. Do you know what I mean?
Yeah. But we survived. Right?
Yeah. But what does that even mean?
See VILJAR's doubt.
INT. ILA PRISON. BREIVIK'S CELL - SAME TIME
BREIVIK in his cell...
EXT/INT. COURTHOUSE - JUDGES CHAMBERS - NIGHT
LIPPESTAD up the front steps. Looking concerned. We follow him into the chambers. He sees the Prosecution lawyers INGA and SVEINN. And FAMILY LAWYER. The JUDGE enters.
Your Honour, the families welcome this decision. And they thank you. We will call our own psychiatric experts to give evidence in the trial.
(standing) Your Honour, the court has already had Mr. Breivik psychiatrically examined. Allowing further assessment has no precedent in law.
Mr, Lippestad, the court is trying to do what's best for Norway. See LIPPESTAD. This is a reverse.
INT. ILA PRISON - HOLDING ROOM - DAY
LIPPESTAD watching BREIVIK reading the Court's judgement. Why are they doing this?
They want you to answer for what you did.
But this is my trial.
Victims have rights too. They have to be able to call their witnesses and testify, just as you can. It's their right, Anders.
Hold on BREIVIK, angry at being checked for the first time.
INT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - VILJAR'S NEW ROOM - DAY
VILJAR packing his things into a bag (now with a stick). Sudden silent shards of memory: ANDERS and SIMON's faces, laughing as they unpack by their tents.
EXT. ULLEVAL HOSPITAL - MAIN ENTRANCE - DAY
LARA watches from the curb, as VILJAR is helped into the back of a people-carrier. He sits next to TORJE. TORJE quickly looks away.
Hi! I wanted to see you off. Will you be alright?
Yeah. It's gonna be good to get home. What about you? Will you be OK?
Yep. I'll be fine. I have to look for a job to keep myself busy, so I don't think about things.
SVEINN walks over to them.
You too. (to VILJAR) I'll call you.
CHRISTIN comes up and says goodbye to LARA. Viljar gets into back of the van.
We'll have that race when you get back to Oslo then.
I'm going to do some serious training and then I'll beat you!
CHRISTIN closes the door and gets into the car. LARA watches the car pull away.
EXT. SVALBARD - AERIAL - DAY
A remote island in the ARCTIC SEA. 2000km from OSLO. Snow and mountain wilderness. A kind of Eden.
INT. HANSSEN CAR - CONTINUOUS
VILJAR and FAMILY drive towards the small community of
EXT/INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - DAY
They pull up outside a small clapper-board home. A snowmobile parked outside. VILJAR gets out of the car. He struggles to walk. They enter and CHRISTIN helps VIlJAR with his coat.
Do you want me to...
Be careful.

TORJE goes ahead of him to his bedroom and shuts the door. SVEINN takes the cases upstairs. Alone in his bedroom, VILJAR sees the life he once had. Smiling photos with his friends, his Arsenal posters, his skis.
Do you want me to help with your shoes?
No, I can do it.
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - LIVING ROOM - CONTINUOUS 165
Meanwhile upstairs, CHRISTIN unpacks. SVEINN can feel her tension. He catches her. She stifles tears.
Breath with me. In - out.
She breaths deeply.
INT. SVALBARD. VILJAR'S HOME - LATER
TORJE in his bedroom, headphones on. VILJAR in his bedroom, sees a car approach. A knock at the front door. CHRISTIN answers it. SIMON and ANDERS' PARENTS, last seen at the hotel. A terrible moment.
(holds out a bottle) Simon and Anders would have wanted us to welcome you home.
I'm so sorry.
They embrace. Conflicted in grief and love.
INT. ILA PRISON - BREIVIK'S CELL - DAY
BREIVIK angrily pacing. Deep in thought.
EXT. PLAY SCHOOL - DAY
Establisher of playschool. KIDS play outside.
INT. PLAY SCHOOL - EMPTY CLASSROOM - CONTINUOUS
SIGNE and LIPPESTAD in a meeting with HEADMISTRESS.
There have been complaints. From parents. Because you're defending such a dangerous man. Meaning?
They're worried about the school being targeted.
It's a very difficult time. We have to think about what's best for everyone. Seriously?
I'm sorry. But you need to find another school.
EXT. PLAY SCHOOL - STREET - CONTINUOUS
LIPPESTAD and SIGNE leaving the school, with their DAUGHTER.
There's Frogner down by the museum. At least it's nearer.
Is it good?
It's okay. It wasn't the one we chose. (suddenly she breaks down) I'm sorry. I'm sorry.
LIPPESTAD holds her.
INT. ILA PRISON CORRIDOR - NIGHT
LIPPESTAD is led by a GUARD down the corridor...
INT. ILA PRISON - HOLDING ROOM - NIGHT
I want to change my plea. I'm not insane.
(hasn't seen this coming) But you agreed to this defense. The psychiatric assessment said you were ill.

I was playing a role. I was playing the monster in a nightmare. But I'm not a monster. I'm a soldier in a war, doing my duty. A leader. And a leader does not give away his power. I've allowed the families to take control, but this is my trial. I decide how it ends. I have to prove these attacks meant something. (beat) And I need to be judged for what I did.
You'll go to prison. For the rest of your life.
I have to finish what I started. Standing up in the court. I have to stand up for what I believe in. That's the third attack. That's the whole point of this. That is how I win.
EXT. ILA PRISON - NIGHT
LIPPESTAD walks away from the prison.
INT. LIPPESTAD'S OFFICE - DAY
Piles of files across every surface. LIPPESTAD looks tired.
(to his TEAM) He wants to change his defense. He wants to plead guilty.
Has he given any reason? I mean, why?
He wants to stay in control. And he knows the consequences?

He wants to stand up and be accountable for his actions. It's going to be very hard. The psychiatric assessment was clear. He's been diagnosed insane. And the Prosecution are legally obliged to stick with it. So how do we get around that?

Ok so we have to start again. To show that he knew what he was doing. Every step of the way. That only someone rational could have planned and executed something this complex. And we have to look into the Far-Right. Find witnesses who share his beliefs. So we can show that he's not alone in his views. That's the only way we can do this.
EXT. SVALBARD - DAY Wide shot of Longyearbyen.
Hemmed in by mountains on one side, and the Arctic Sea on the other. A pick-up pulls up at a remote building. SVEINN gets out.
INT. SVALBARD MAYOR'S OFFICE - DAY
CHRISTIN in her new office with her TEAM.

(to the group) We need to generate new ideas. New ways of bringing investment back into the local economy. Coal prices are falling. We can't rely on the mines anymore. Fishing too. It's up to us to look for new ways to secure Svalbard's future.
INT. SVEINN'S OFFICE - DAY
SVEINN in his more spartan office space. A couple of computers. Science equipment, outdoor gear, charts tracking migration patterns across the Arctic Sea. See SVEINN, trying to engage with his work.
INT. SVALBARD HOSPITAL - MRI UNIT - DAY
VILJAR lying in an MRI tunnel. The screen showing his damaged brain.
INT. SVALBARD HOSPITAL - EXAMINATION ROOM - DAY
An EYE DOCTOR carefully fits a prosthetic eye into VILJAR'S empty socket. Bright blue, but lifeless.
INT. SVALBARD HOSPITAL - DOCTOR'S ROOM - DAY
VILJAR in front of a SECOND DOCTOR.
(he shrugs) My head hurts. All the time. And my shoulder. (beat) I get phantom pain... That's natural.
See VILJAR, it doesn't feel natural to him.
(cuts him off) I know what the fucking dangers are.
EXT. SVALBARD - STREET - DAY
VILJAR walking painfully back from the hospital. A sudden noise - he flinches, frightened.
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - BATHROOM - DAY

Close up in a mirror: VILJAR, topless, looks at his false eye. The red scars across his skull, shoulder. His deformed hand. In the background: BREIVIK is on TV. Dominating the headlines. Holding the country to ransom. Hold on VILJAR. No escape. As we track in, we feel his anger rising. He punches the mirror.
EXT. VILJAR'S HOME - CONTINUOUS
VILJAR slewing his snow mobile down the hill.
INT. VILJAR'S HOME - SAME TIME
TORJE runs up the stairs Mum, Dad! CHRISTIN and SVEINN look out the window. See VILJAR speeding away. They run down to the pick-up.
EXT. SVALBARD - SNOW - SNOWMOBILES - NIGHT

A POV at speed through dense snow. VILJAR on the snow mobile. Riding hard. Back to the POV again: Round a bend. See Viljar pushing on. Faster, faster. As if daring to crash. Now lights behind... SVEINN and CHRISTIN coming after him in a pick-up. Desperate to catch him. VILJAR turns a sharp bend. Faster still. Another bend. And another. See VILJAR, closing on the trees. Closing, closing... until at the last minute he ploughs into a soft snow drift.
(out of the pickup) Viljar!!
He and CHRISTIN run over to him. VILJAR walking away. Wait!
I can't live like this! Thinking I'm going to get better. I'm not. It's all still here. In my head. I can't get rid of it.
They get closer. He turns.
What the fuck am I supposed to do? Tell me! Tell me!
I don't know. I wish it had been me not you.
(hugs him) It's OK. Let's go home, OK.
INT. LIPPESTAD'S HOME - NIGHT

LIPPESTAD searches Far-Right forums online: Fascism, racism, violence. Ancient hatreds spewing out of his computer, pouring out across Europe, across the world. See SIGNE watching, heavily pregnant, her unease impossible to hide.
EXT. EAST OSLO - EXTREMIST' HOUSE - DAY

A part of Oslo we haven't seen before. Poorer, harsher. Neglected. LIPPESTAD parks outside a block of flats. A huddle of MUSLIM MEN on chairs outside a corner shop. LIPPESTAD heads for a tower block. Sees Far-Right graffiti. The Norwegian flag. KIDS faces through the windows. He enters the block and gets into lift. Doors close.
EXTREMIST'S APARTMENT - DAY
LIPPESTAD sits in front of a white man (40s).
My client Anders Breivik writes about you in his manifesto. He obviously admires you.
Many people admire me. But it's not me they admire. It's my ideas about the world.
Did you ever meet him?
He asked. But I'm careful who I work with. What I do, who I meet. (beat) He's not from round here you know. He's from your end of town. But yes, we share the same vision for the world.
He says a war is coming. I'd say it's already started...
Are you a member of the Knight's Templar?
There are many other organisations.
The Nordic Resistance Movement?
All across Scandinavia. Northern Europe. We're strong in eastern Europe too of course. Very strong in the UK too. New groups forming all the time.
How about America?
Stormfront. Growing fast. Very fast. And joining up with us here in Europe.

How do you communicate? Where you can't see us. In chat rooms. The Dark Web. Lippestad, with every al-Qaeda attack, we grow in strength. Every migrant boat that crosses the Med, more people join. There's a lot of fear and anger out there... And that's why, tomorrow will belong to us. That hangs there.
Will you testify in my client's defense?
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - KITCHEN - DAY
CHRISTIN receives an email. Her expression changes. Goes downstairs. Puts on coat.
(seeing VILJAR) You OK?
I'm just going to see your father.
VILJAR watches his MOTHER drive away.
EXT. SVALBARD - SVEINN'S OFFICE - DAY
SVEINN tagging birds in cages, 400m from his office. CHRISTIN arrives.
We just got an email. From the lawyers. There's a briefing on the mainland. They want us to go. So we know what to expect from the trial. You should go.
What?
We both need to engage with this. It's important. Engaging nearly cost our sons their lives.

They're still here. Are they? (beat) I don't care about him. I don't care about Anders fucking Breivik and his trial. And I just want us to get on with our lives. That's all. Just that. We've got them home. I just want our boys back. And I don't want any trial stopping it.
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - LIVING ROOM - DAY
VILJAR alone. On TV: relentless coverage of Breivik's imminent trial. He turns it off.
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - UPSTAIRS HALLWAY - DAY
VILJAR outside Torje's closed door. Loud music from inside.
(no answer) I'm going into town. Do you want to come?
EXT. SVALBARD - MAIN STREET - DAY
VILJAR walking slowly along Longyearbyen's main street. FAMILIES, KIDS. A festive atmosphere. The sun is about to return. VILJAR walks on, head down.
INT. SVALBARD - CAFE - CONTINUOUS
VILJAR at a table alone. Watching STUDENTS on the street outside, laughing. Then:
VOICE (O.S.)
The voice is on Viljar's blind side. Panic. And then he sees: SIMON'S FATHER.
That's OK. I don't want to disturb you. It's fine.
How are you?
I'm OK. It's taking a while. You know... I'm sorry. I meant to visit. How are you? How's Tone?
It's tough. Yeah.
Listen, we're going ice-fishing this weekend. To Simon's spot. Would you like to come? There's plenty of room in the cabin.
Thanks... I... Can I let you know? Sure.
He looks disappointed.
Sorry. I will come. I'm just... It's just a bit...
It's OK. I understand.
Viljar stands, painfully. He leaves.
EXT. UYOYA ISLAND - VILJAR SHORE - FLASHBACK
VILJAR lying on the rocks, bleeding out. Simon? No reply. Only distant gunfire. Muffled cries of pain on the wind.
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - LATER
VILJAR closes the front door behind him. Sees TORJE at the end of the corridor.
Do you want to watch a movie or something?
Sure. Maybe later. I'm just doing some music.
TORJE goes into his room. Closes his door. VILJAR goes to TORJE's door.
Torje, talk to me.
I'm fine.
No you're not. You know you can talk to me.
I don't need to. You're the one who got hurt, not me.
You were there too.
See TORJE, remembering the island.
I'm sorry that Mum and Dad have been looking after me so much. But we're home now. It's going to be OK.
It hangs there. VILJAR walks away.
EXT. TROMSO ESTABLISHER (AIRPORT TOWN) INT. TROMSO - HOTEL/MEETING ROOM - DAY
Follow CHRISTIN towards a room, crowded with PARENTS and their LAWYERS. She pauses outside. Hears a loud argument in progress. She opens the door and slips into the back as the argument rages.
My daughter can't leave her bedroom because of him. He broke her, and now he gets to stand up there and tell us how he did it.
I will be there to ensure that doesn't happen. But it is happening. That's exactly
what he's doing.
Look, the judge is letting us bring our own psychiatric experts, and we're closing the trial with victim impact statements- They should just hang him.
Listen, I promise you, we will have our chance to- But it's not enough! This isn't justice! They should just string him up. Be done with it.
See CHRISTIN. Finally she can't take it anymore.

(to everyone) Our children won't get justice by executing him. (beat) I know you're angry. I'm angry too. And my sons came back. Many of your children didn't. But we can't surrender to our anger, no matter how much we feel it. Somehow we have to learn to trust the world again. To trust each other. We have to reconnect. And that begins when we walk into that courtroom. (beat) Our power will come from how we respond to this man. Not in how we silence him. We have to trust in justice. That's the only way we'll win.
EXT. TROMSO - HOTEL - LATER
CHRISTIN by her car. She hands CHRISTIN a letter.
(beat) I would like him to testify, if you think he's up to it?
I'll give it to him.
EXT. SVALBARD - STREET - NIGHT
VILJAR walks away from the house. Opens the envelope. Reads.
They want him to testify...
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - LIVING ROOM - LATER
Angle on VILJAR out of the window. TV on in living room.
get the doctor to excuse him.
Maybe he needs to.
What does that mean?
Maybe he needs to face it.
How do we know it won't set him back?
We don't. It's his choice.
EXT. SVALBARD - STREET - BEDROOM - SAME TIME
VILJAR gets out phone and dials.
Hey. I was worried about you.
I'm fine... (beat) I just received a letter. They want me to appear. At the trial.
On Viljar, flash of relief.
Do you think you can face him? Suddenly we're back on the island. Gunfire. Screaming. And
the DARK FIGURE looming above. VILJAR forces himself back.
Yeah. I'm here. I said, do you think you can do it?
I can't even walk down the fucking street.
Maybe we need to try.
Yeah... I need to think about it. I'll call you later. Bye. See VILJAR alone.
EXT. OSLO - DAWN Oslo at sunrise. EXT. ILA PRISON - DAY
Breivik's white van convoy leaves through the prison gates.
EXT. COURTHOUSE - DAY 1 - DAY
ANGRY CROWDS mass behind barriers. ARMED POLICE, SNIFFER DOGS. And the world's MEDIA too.
INT. COURTROOM - DAY 1 - DAY

Follow LIPPESTAD into the empty courtroom. A moment on his Then the prosecution lawyers, INGA and SVEINN enter. Then the FAMILY LAWYER and her team. The soft shuffle of legal papers, as we hear the gathering sound of an angry roar outside.
EXT. COURTHOUSE - UNDERGROUND CAR PARK - DAY 1 - SAME TIME204
BREIVIK arrives through a gauntlet of rage. Into the underground car park...
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR' HOME - SAME TIME
VILJAR turns on the TV.
INT. PM OFFICE - SAME TIME
He turns on the TV.
INT. COURTHOUSE - STAIRWELL - DAY 1 - CONTINUOUS
Breivik is led up through the building...
INT. COURTROOM - DAY 1 - CONTINUOUS

LIPPESTAD waits for Breivik to arrive. The courtroom is crowded. Banks of PRESS and CAMERAS. Broadcasting live. He looks to his right: PARENTS, just a few feet away. Those who had argued with CHRISTIN. A ripple through the room as BREIVIK enters. LIPPESTAD watches as he is uncuffed. Turns to face the crowded courtroom. Looks at the parents. Going from face-toface. Then turns and punches the air with a Nazi salute. The room recoils with shock. Cameras click. See LIPPESTAD, he knows the third attack is a call to arms.
INT. SVALBARD - VILJAR'S HOME - CONTINUOUS
VILJAR watches Breivik's salute.
INT. COURTROOM - DAY 1 - DAY
The JUDGE nods to the Prosecution.
LIPPESTAD watches the PARENTS, as they impassively listen.
INGA sits. The JUDGE nods to LIPPESTAD.

(standing) Your Honour, we will not seek to deny the crimes committed on July 22nd. However, we will argue that Mr. Breivik was not psychotic during these attacks. Instead, we say he knew exactly what he was doing. (feels Breivik's eyes on him) And though it is difficult to comprehend Anders Breivik's view of the world, we will show that he is not alone in his opinions. There are others who think the way he does. Increasing numbers of people, all over the west. His were political acts.
INT. COURTROOM - DAY 2 - DAY
BREIVIK performs another Nazi salute.

(his opening statement) I stand here today on behalf of Europeans who are being deprived of their ethnic, indigenous, cultural, and territorial rights. (beat) The Prosecution tell you I am insane. They do this because they fear me. Because I have committed the most sophisticated, the most spectacular, political assassination in Europe since World War Two. (beat, he turns a page) And why?
